description: Conformance vectors for the i18n structural translation-map KEYING (whole-element model). The framework's extractor is correct iff, for each case's `doc`, the set of translation-unit keys it emits equals `expectedKeys`. Pinning the keying keeps translation units stable across releases and across any independent implementation of the same rules. Authority: src/i18n/extract.js (elementText / extractFromProseMirrorDoc).
model_note: WHOLE-ELEMENT keys. One translatable unit per block element; links stay INLINE (NOT split into a separate key). The map VALUE (out of scope for this fixture — it tests keys only) is the translated inline content; the framework serializes it as inline markdown in locales/*.json.

key_rules.input: Keys derive from the ProseMirror doc JSON directly. Node names: heading/paragraph/bulletList/orderedList/listItem/text; marks: link/bold/italic/strike/code/highlight/textStyle/span.
key_rules.key: An element's cleaned source text: trim leading/trailing, KEEP internal whitespace verbatim. ALL inline marks (incl. links) flatten into the text; inline atom nodes (icon/emoji/math) are skipped, EXCEPT hardBreak, which contributes "\n" so a break does not fuse the words around it. Not a hash (the hash is only the file-side join key).
key_rules.links: Links are NOT split out — link text stays inline in its element's key (whole-element keying). This applies uniformly to paragraphs AND list items (vectors E and H).
key_rules.dedup: Keys that normalize-equal (trim + collapse internal whitespace) collapse to one entry; first occurrence's spelling wins.
key_rules.ordering: Document walk order. Compare as a set if your producer orders differently.

cases.rule: bold/italic inside a paragraph flatten (no marks in the key)
link in a paragraph stays INLINE (whole-element) -> ONE key including the link text, no split, no gap
span marks are stripped from the key
link inside a list item stays inline too (same whole-element rule as E) -> one key including the link text
hard break inside a paragraph -> ONE key, with the break contributing "\n" (not nothing, not a space) so the words do not fuse
two same-level headings -> TWO keys, not one merged key: the multi-line-title merge is semantic-parser's grouping layer (readHeadingGroup), not the doc, so the doc still holds two heading elements
